proofFeed.label: Sprint Signal
proofFeed.title: Path generator + sprint flow already live
proofFeed.description: Users can explore the graph, generate a path, and create a sprint proof page from a real occupation node.
proofFeed.meta: Next action: connect more project pathways and role signals into the sprint loop.

proofFeed.label: Community Signal
proofFeed.title: Intro to computing foundations already prepared
proofFeed.description: The foundations course gives beginners a clean way to move from setup to first code to first project contribution.
proofFeed.meta: Next action: route more homepage traffic into the foundations repo and course path.

proofFeed.label: Project Signal
proofFeed.title: Three starter project tracks seeded
proofFeed.description: The platform now has beginner, cross-field, and advanced technical directions to create visible movement immediately.
proofFeed.meta: Next action: fill roles, enter sprint, and publish proof updates weekly.

proofFeed.label: Deployed Product
proofFeed.title: Read And Analyze is already live for document workflows
proofFeed.description: Students and professionals can already review documents, extract text, generate questions and notes, and use AI chat in a cleaner interface.

proofFeed.label: Deployed Product
proofFeed.title: Examination Manager and Task Manager are ready for review
proofFeed.description: The community already has deployable education and productivity products people can test, critique, and improve together.

nextActivities.label: Decision Window
nextActivities.title: Run the role-based poll for upcoming calls
nextActivities.description: A poll will be used to decide when each community group should meet so treatment becomes more specific instead of too broad.
nextActivities.meta: Focus groups: Domain Experts, Engineers, Designers / Operators

nextActivities.label: Community Calls
nextActivities.title: Schedule domain-specific calls by role
nextActivities.description: Separate calls will help domain experts, developers, designers, and operators get more relevant discussions and clearer direction.
nextActivities.meta: Priority roles: domain experts, engineers, designers, operators

nextActivities.label: Video Planning
nextActivities.title: Decide which hands-on video to create first
nextActivities.description: The next public media push should choose the most important first video: intro to community, intro to computing, system design, tech for your field, monetization, AI automation, or collaboration systems.
nextActivities.meta: Goal: create a practical first-watch path for new members

nextActivities.label: Live Build
nextActivities.title: Turn the next activity into a visible live system
nextActivities.description: Each chosen activity should be built in public so members can see the system forming, not just hear theory about it.
nextActivities.meta: Outcome: more trust, more proof, more participation

blogPosts.label: Starter Guide
blogPosts.title: How to find your path in the community without getting overwhelmed
blogPosts.description: A plain-language guide for beginners and domain experts on how to choose a role, a repo, and a first visible action.
blogPosts.meta: Best for: new members, career explorers, multidisciplinary joiners

blogPosts.label: Field Guide
blogPosts.title: How to use technology to strengthen the field you already belong to
blogPosts.description: A practical explanation for healthcare, education, business, law, design, and other non-software fields on where tech creates leverage.
blogPosts.meta: Best for: domain experts and non-tech professionals

blogPosts.label: Build Guide
blogPosts.title: How to learn effectively, build proof, and move toward monetization
blogPosts.description: A post about turning scattered learning into a repeatable loop: learn, build, document, publish, and use your work to create opportunity.
blogPosts.meta: Best for: builders, freelancers, founders, and serious learners

blogPosts.label: AI Guide
blogPosts.title: How AI and automation can help you do more with less manual work
blogPosts.description: A practical post on using machines to automate repeated work, support decision-making, and build stronger systems across fields.
blogPosts.meta: Best for: operations, analytics, automation, and product-thinking

deployedProducts.label: APP + WEB
deployedProducts.title: Read And Analyze
deployedProducts.description: Read documents, process content, extract text, generate questions and notes, and use AI chat with a stronger UI.
deployedProducts.meta: Review it and send improvement ideas back into the community.

deployedProducts.label: WEB
deployedProducts.title: Examination Manager
deployedProducts.description: Take CBT exams, prepare for tests, generate questions, and shape it into an app if the community demands it.
deployedProducts.meta: Current deployment is web-first, app version can follow.

deployedProducts.label: APP + WEB
deployedProducts.title: Task Manager
deployedProducts.description: Manage activities, start, stop, pause, continue later, and share task flows for better productivity.
deployedProducts.meta: Use the Firebase app or the Netlify surface depending on what you want to test.

deployedProducts.label: LIVE SURFACE
deployedProducts.title: EruditeWBT Netlify app surface
deployedProducts.description: Open the broader live product surface and use it as an entry point for community testing.
deployedProducts.meta: This is a good route for public demos and quick validation.
